ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic tart category (FOODON_03301116). No specific code exists for mango pomelo tart with almond cream and honey mochi. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pastries and cakes) is the closest defensible parent category for this composite fruit tart. No specific code exists for mango pomelo tart.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific composite dessert tart product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One piece of mango pomelo tart weighing approximately 120 g, as typically served at a Singaporean premium dessert café.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fruit tart, with custard or cream filling (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite); supplemented with USDA entries for mango, pomelo, almond cream (frangipane), mochi, and pastry shell components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for mango pomelo tart with almond cream and honey mochi exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central entries: pastry shell (FDC 172764), almond cream/frangipane (FDC 168594 almond paste as proxy), honey mochi (FDC 169717 mochi proxy), fresh mango (FDC 169910), pomelo (FDC 167762), and mango curd (estimated from lemon curd FDC 172157 with mango substitution). Approximate component weight distribution per 100 g finished tart: pastry shell 25 g, almond cream 20 g, honey mochi 15 g, fresh mango 20 g, pomelo 10 g, mango curd 10 g. Values are estimates and carry medium confidence.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of fruit tarts and pastry-based desserts — analogous estimate based on fruit tart (GI ~55) from University of Sydney GI Database; supplemented by Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load, Diabetes Care
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for mango pomelo tart with almond cream and honey mochi. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous fruit tart entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. The pastry shell and mango curd contribute moderate glycemic load; fresh mango GI is approximately 51 (low-medium, University of Sydney GI Database); pomelo GI is approximately 25 (low). Honey mochi contributes higher GI (~65–70 for mochi/glutinous rice products per Atkinson et al. 2021). Blended estimate of 55 is defensible for this composite dessert. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 45.0) / 100 = 24.8 g, using serving size 120 g and carbohydrate_g per 100 g of 37.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~114 mg per serving) and low saturated fat; fruit components provide potassium
hypertension.guidance: One piece (120 g) contributes approximately 114 mg sodium, which is modest within a 2000 mg daily sodium limit; individuals with hypertension may consume this dessert occasionally but should account for total daily sodium intake from other sources.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~24 g per serving) and low glycemic index (55) with a glycemic load of approximately 24.8 g per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 24.8 g per serving and approximately 24 g of sugar per piece, this dessert poses a significant postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and should be consumed only occasionally in small portions with careful blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~5.9 g per serving) from almond cream and pastry shell, partially offset by beneficial monounsaturated fats from almonds and vitamin C from mango and pomelo
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The almond cream and pastry shell contribute approximately 5.9 g of saturated fat per serving, which represents a meaningful proportion of the recommended daily limit of less than 20 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional treats and prioritise lower-saturated-fat dessert alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Mango Pomelo Tart with Almond Cream and Honey Mochi is a premium Singaporean contemporary dessert tart sold at specialty cafés such as Kazo Premium. It consists of a baked shortcrust or sweet pastry shell filled with silky almond cream (frangipane), topped with a layer of honey-sweetened mochi, fresh sliced mango, segments of zesty pomelo, and a glossy mango curd. The dessert combines French patisserie technique with East Asian ingredients — the almond cream provides a rich, nutty, custard-like base, while the honey mochi adds a chewy, lightly sweet textural contrast. Fresh mango delivers tropical sweetness and juiciness, pomelo contributes a refreshing citrus tartness, and the mango curd ties the layers together with an intense, smooth mango flavour. The tart is typically served chilled or at room temperature. Nutritionally, one piece (approximately 120 g) provides around 322 kcal, 45 g of carbohydrate, 24 g of sugar, 14 g of fat, and 5 g of protein. The low glycemic index (approximately 55) but high glycemic load (~24.8 g per serving) makes this dessert a significant postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its overall carbohydrate and sugar content. The saturated fat content from almond cream and pastry is moderate and relevant for cardiovascular health. Mango and pomelo contribute vitamin C, vitamin A, and dietary fibre. This dessert is vegetarian and likely halal, but contains gluten, eggs, dairy, and tree nuts (almonds), making it unsuitable for those with these allergens.

Mango pomelo tart GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific composite dessert tart exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central approved source entries for individual components (pastry shell, almond cream, mochi, mango, pomelo, mango curd). GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database fruit tart data. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA sources are high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates; no exact HPB or USDA entry exists for this specific product. All component sources are from USDA FoodData Central (approved secondary global source). 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not meaningfully reported in the USDA component sources for this dessert type and is not expected to be a significant contributor. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA component sources used for this estimation. 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. The estimate is based on fruit tart analogues and component GI values (mango ~51, pomelo ~25, mochi ~65–70, pastry ~55–60). 5. Trans fat (trans_fat_g = 0.1) reflects a small residual from pastry shortening; this is a low-level estimate. 6. Halal status is assumed likely based on ingredients but not confirmed by restaurant certification. 7. Serving size of 120 g is an estimate for one premium dessert tart piece; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 8. This record was generated from a GrabFood Singapore seed record (GD001978) for Kazo Premium - Funan and has been fully processed under Project Veritas v2.
